Title: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana for rapid functional analysis of genes involved in non-photochemical quenching and carotenoid biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEARLEYGATE 100 expression vector; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text (NA)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to accelerate and amplify qE/NPQ in crops under fluctuating light. Engineering natural PSBS variants or sequence-guided edits in lumen-facing proton-sensing regions could tune activation/relaxation kinetics; should be combined with xanthophyll-cycle engineering for stronger photoprotection optimization.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of AtPSBS markedly increased NPQ capacity in N. benthamiana leaf spots.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient overexpression in N. benthamiana; chlorophyll fluorescence NPQ measurements; immunoblot detection of FLAG-tagged protein; pigment profiling by HPLC.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light treatment (~600 μmol photons m^-2 s^-1) and dark recovery after dark acclimation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in fully expanded leaves.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PSBS is described as a modulator of NPQ rather than a carotenoid biosynthetic enzyme. Its qE effect depends on protonation-sensitive lumen-exposed glutamates.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious penalty reported for AtPSBS in this transient assay.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Photosystem II subunit S (PSBS), light-harvesting-complex-related thylakoid protein
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Transient AtPSBS expression strongly increased NPQ capacity in mature N. benthamiana leaves. 2. The result reproduced known PSBS-dependent qE enhancement seen in stable systems. 3. The study supports PSBS tuning as a route to engineer photoprotective energy dissipation.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dissipates excess absorbed light energy and protects photosystems from photodamage.

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEARLEYGATE 100 expression vector; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text (NA)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: PpPSBS is a promising allelic resource for crop engineering because it conferred stronger qE than AtPSBS despite lower accumulation; useful for optimizing photoprotection under dynamic light.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PpPSBS conferred the highest levels of qE among tested PSBS orthologs in N. benthamiana.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression of orthologs and protonation-insensitive mutants; NPQ and ΦPSII measurements; immunoblotting.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light exposure (~600 μmol photons m^-2 s^-1) and dark recovery.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Enhanced qE required conserved lumen-exposed glutamates; lower protein abundance still produced stronger quenching than AtPSBS.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear adverse effect reported in this assay.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Photosystem II subunit S (PSBS), light-harvesting-complex-related thylakoid protein
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpPSBS enhanced qE in planta. 2. Its activity depended on conserved protonatable glutamates. 3. It outperformed AtPSBS despite lower accumulation, highlighting useful natural variation for engineering.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photoprotection through rapid dissipation of excess excitation energy.

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEARLEYGATE 100 expression vector; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text (NA)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CrPSBS provides a distinct ortholog for tuning NPQ kinetics, but its partial negative effect on PSII efficiency suggests caution before crop deployment.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CrPSBS conferred protonation-dependent qE enhancement in N. benthamiana, but to a lesser extent than AtPSBS or PpPSBS, and slightly reduced Fv/Fm.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression of wild-type and protonation-insensitive double mutant; NPQ and ΦPSII measurements; Fv/Fm assessment; immunoblotting.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light exposure (~600 μmol photons m^-2 s^-1) and dark recovery.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly light induced in C. reinhardtii according to cited transcript studies.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Referenced external transcript studies only; no accession provided in the paper excerpt.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The first 44 aa of CrPSBS were replaced with the AtPSBS transit peptide to ensure chloroplast targeting in planta. The study suggests CrPSBS may have qE-related function despite prior uncertainty in its native species.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slightly reduced Fv/Fm and delayed ΦPSII recovery, suggesting possible membrane-organization disruption when accumulated in higher plants.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Photosystem II subunit S (PSBS), light-harvesting-complex-related thylakoid protein
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrPSBS showed in vivo protonation-dependent qE activity when transiently expressed in N. benthamiana. 2. Activity was weaker than PpPSBS and AtPSBS. 3. CrPSBS also lowered PSII efficiency metrics, indicating possible side effects of heterologous accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEARLEYGATE 100 expression vector; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text (NA)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong target to accelerate NPQ induction by pre-accumulating zeaxanthin; can be stacked with PSBS or other NPQ components, but excessive constitutive activity may perturb pigment homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: De-epoxidizes violaxanthin to zeaxanthin via antheraxanthin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtVDE overexpression caused rapid NPQ induction and markedly increased zeaxanthin accumulation, including under low light/dark-acclimated conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression; NPQ kinetics; HPLC pigment profiling before and after high light; DES calculation; immunoblotting.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the target photoprotective xanthophyll zeaxanthin.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light (~600 μmol photons m^-2 s^-1), dark acclimation, dark recovery.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in fully expanded leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MGDG (membrane lipid, as functional association mentioned)
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream branch-cycle step / reversible xanthophyll-cycle step
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unexpected zeaxanthin accumulation under low light/dark may reflect background lumen acidification or higher background activity due to overabundant enzyme in the thylakoid lumen.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive zeaxanthin accumulation may alter pigment balance; no growth penalty assessed in transient assay.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for rapid zeaxanthin formation and NPQ induction
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast thylakoid lumen / membrane-associated upon activation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtVDE overexpression accelerated NPQ induction in N. benthamiana. 2. It lowered violaxanthin and increased zeaxanthin, even before high light treatment. 3. Elevated DES correlated with faster induction, confirming functional VDE activity in the heterologous system.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports qE and qZ components of NPQ and photoprotection.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEARLEYGATE 100 expression vector; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text (NA)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to tune slower NPQ induction or faster relaxation via zeaxanthin consumption; deployment must be balanced because excessive activity suppresses photoprotective zeaxanthin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Epoxidizes zeaxanthin back to violaxanthin via antheraxanthin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtZEP overexpression slowed NPQ induction, prevented strong zeaxanthin accumulation under high light, and increased relaxation speed.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression; NPQ measurements; HPLC pigment profiling; DES calculation; immunoblotting.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target photoprotective zeaxanthin accumulation by converting it back toward violaxanthin.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light (~600 μmol photons m^-2 s^-1), dark acclimation, dark recovery.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in fully expanded leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream branch-cycle step / reversible xanthophyll-cycle step
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as constitutively active in contrast to pH-activated VDE.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overactivity may attenuate photoprotective NPQ and increase susceptibility to excess light.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key opposing control step for NPQ relaxation and zeaxanthin turnover
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtZEP overexpression suppressed high-light zeaxanthin accumulation. 2. NPQ induction became slower and did not reach control levels within the assay window. 3. The transient system faithfully reproduced the antagonistic effect of ZEP versus VDE on the xanthophyll cycle.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls photoprotection versus light-harvesting balance through xanthophyll cycling.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEARLEYGATE 100 expression vector; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text (NA)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Validated algal VDE ortholog for engineering faster NPQ induction using alternative enzyme kinetics or pH sensitivity distinct from plant VDE.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: De-epoxidizes violaxanthin to zeaxanthin via antheraxanthin in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NoVDE expression accelerated NPQ induction and increased zeaxanthin after high light, indicating functional VDE activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression; NPQ/ΦPSII measurements; HPLC pigment analysis; DES calculation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms zeaxanthin that supports NPQ.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light (~600 μmol photons m^-2 s^-1), dark acclimation, dark recovery.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein was not detected by immunoblot, but phenotype and pigment changes support functional accumulation below detection or C-terminal processing.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major pigment perturbation in dark; engineering outcome may depend on lumen pH regulation distinct from AtVDE.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for inducible zeaxanthin production under light
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast-associated; transit peptide engineering used in some algal constructs to ensure plastid localization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NoVDE accelerated NPQ induction in N. benthamiana similarly to AtVDE. 2. It produced more zeaxanthin than control after high light but little before illumination. 3. Results support annotation of NoVDE as a functional VDE with kinetic behavior distinct from AtVDE.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photoprotection via activation of NPQ under excess light.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEARLEYGATE 100 expression vector; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text (NA)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A diatom de-epoxidase providing an alternative catalytic module for faster NPQ induction; useful for comparative engineering of NPQ kinetics.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: De-epoxidizes violaxanthin-cycle substrate(s) to increase zeaxanthin accumulation in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TpDDE accelerated NPQ induction and increased zeaxanthin after high light, consistent with de-epoxidase function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression; NPQ/ΦPSII measurements; HPLC pigment analysis; DES calculation; immunoblotting.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly increases de-epoxidized xanthophyll pool supporting NPQ.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light (~600 μmol photons m^-2 s^-1), dark acclimation, dark recovery.
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Diadinoxanthin de-epoxidase-like de-epoxidase (homolog of plant VDE)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although sourced from a diatom de-epoxidase homolog, it could use violaxanthin in the plant background and behaved analogously to VDE in the heterologous assay.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty reported; exact native substrate range in diatom context may differ.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key inducible de-epoxidation step in this assay
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast-associated; transit peptide engineering used for proper plastid localization in some algal constructs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TpDDE expression sped NPQ induction in N. benthamiana. 2. It elevated high-light zeaxanthin and DES relative to control. 3. The results validate TpDDE as a functional de-epoxidase in planta.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEARLEYGATE 100 expression vector; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text (NA)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to enlarge the β-xanthophyll pool and create novel NPQ kinetic states, but deployment should consider delayed relaxation and reduced β-carotene.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates β-carotene toward β-xanthophylls, increasing violaxanthin/zeaxanthin pool and decreasing β-carotene.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NoCYP97F5 increased β-xanthophyll accumulation about twofold, reduced β-carotene by ~25%, altered NPQ shape, and delayed NPQ/ΦPSII recovery.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression; NPQ/ΦPSII measurements; HPLC pigment profiling before/after high light; immunoblotting.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and diverts flux toward β-xanthophyll accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light (~600 μmol photons m^-2 s^-1), dark acclimation, dark recovery.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream branch step supplying xanthophyll pool
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper validates NoCYP97F5 as the only predicted β-carotene hydroxylase in N. oceanica and shows it functions alongside native N. benthamiana hydroxylases. The text likely contains a typo referring once to NoCYP97C; the core tested gene is NoCYP97F5.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Delayed NPQ relaxation and delayed ΦPSII recovery; reduced β-carotene pool.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450, CYP97 family carotene hydroxylase
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting; acts as a flux-modifying branch enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast-associated; transit peptide engineering used for plastid localization in some algal constructs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NoCYP97F5 expression increased the β-xanthophyll pool and reduced β-carotene. 2. Despite higher zeaxanthin formation after high light, NPQ amplitude was not enhanced and recovery was delayed. 3. The data validate NoCYP97F5 as a functional β-carotene hydroxylase with distinct physiological effects on photoprotection.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photoprotection, light harvesting modulation, and NPQ-related energy dissipation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEARLEYGATE 100 expression vector; promoter not explicitly stated in provided text (NA)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for engineering novel pigment profiles, especially lutein epoxide accumulation and altered NPQ induction; caution required because it lowers lutein and slows initial photoprotection.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Epoxidizes zeaxanthin-cycle substrates and also epoxidizes lutein to lutein epoxide in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NoZEP1 slowed NPQ induction, reduced lutein, produced lutein epoxide as a new pigment, and showed epoxidase activity on available substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression; NPQ/ΦPSII measurements; HPLC pigment profiling; comparison with cucumber lutein epoxide standard; immunoblotting.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with zeaxanthin accumulation and creates a new modified xanthophyll pool affecting NPQ kinetics.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light (~600 μmol photons m^-2 s^-1) and dark recovery; dark acclimation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Endogenous NbVDE functionally counteracts NoZEP1 product by converting lutein epoxide/violaxanthin-related pools during high light
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream reversible xanthophyll-cycle and side-route epoxidation step
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only NoZEP1, not NoZEP2, showed clear epoxidase activity in this assay. NoZEP1 appears unable to distinguish β-rings of α- and β-carotenoid-derived xanthophylls, epoxidizing both zeaxanthin and lutein-derived substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: VAZ cycle plus lutein–lutein epoxide (L-Lx) cycle
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced lutein content before illumination can impair rapid qE induction; altered pigment composition may affect light harvesting.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of pigment profile and NPQ induction kinetics in this assay
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast-associated; transit peptide engineering used for plastid localization in some algal constructs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NoZEP1 is a functional epoxidase in planta and the likely VAZ-cycle ZEP in N. oceanica. 2. It generated lutein epoxide and markedly reduced lutein, creating a novel pigment profile. 3. These pigment shifts delayed early NPQ induction but allowed later recovery toward control NPQ levels under high light.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Modulates photoprotection and light-harvesting properties through pigment composition changes.
